explanation: This structured natural language property expresses that in both PCV and PSV ventilation modes, when the user sets an Expiratory Pause via the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), the ventilator waits until the current expiratory phase ends (expiratoryPhaseEnd). If the Expiratory Pause is still set (expiratoryPauseButton remains true), then both the inspiratory and expiratory valves are to be closed (inValveClose and outValveClose) until the GUI stops the Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPauseButton becomes false). This captures the conditional, mode-scoped, event-driven behavior described in the requirement.

decision3_substring: both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until the expiratory pause is stopped by the GUI.

explanation:
The input requirement specifies behavior in PCV and PSV modes: If the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then after the next expiration phase ends, and as long as the Expiratory Pause is still required, both inspiratory and expiratory valves must remain closed until the pause is stopped by the GUI. The relevant atomic propositions are: PCVMode and PSVMode for the mode context; expiratoryPauseButton for the GUI command to start pause; expiratoryPause to indicate the pause is active; inValveClose for inspiratory valve close; outValveClose for expiratory valve close.

- decision1: We choose 'while (PCVMode | PSVMode), _ABSTRACT_VAR1_' to confine the property to PCV or PSV modes.
- decision2: We choose 'upon (expiratoryPauseButton), _ABSTRACT_VAR2_' to mark the point when the GUI requests an Expiratory Pause.
- decision3: After the end of the next expiration (expiratoryPhaseEnd), if the pause is still required (expiratoryPause), both valves should close (inValveClose & outValveClose) until the pause is stopped (¬expiratoryPause). This is captured by 'until !expiratoryPause, satisfy (inValveClose & outValveClose)'.

The chosen formalization focuses on the fact that whenever the right trigger conditions are met (being in PCVMode or PSVMode, and having the Expiratory Pause set, and the expiration phase having just ended), the system must eventually close both valves for as long as the Expiratory Pause is active (until it is stopped by the GUI). This captures the sequencing and control dependencies described in the requirement.

explanation: This translation captures the requirement that in PCV and PSV modes, if the Expiratory Pause is requested via the GUI, then after the next expiration phase ends (i.e., after 'expiratoryPhaseEnd'), and if the Expiratory Pause is still required, the ventilator must close both inspiratory and expiratory valves (which can be expressed as 'inValveClose & outValveClose') until the Expiratory Pause is cancelled by the GUI (i.e., 'expiratoryPauseButton' is no longer set). The structured NL property captures this by detecting the condition that in PCV or PSV mode and the Expiratory Pause was requested, then upon the end of the next expiration phase, the inspiratory and expiratory valves should close and remain closed until the Expiratory Pause is no longer active.

explanation: This property encodes the requirement that in PCV or PSV modes, if an expiratory pause is requested by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), the ventilator must wait until the next expiration phase ends (expiratoryPhaseEnd), confirm that the pause is still requested (expiratoryPauseButton), and then close both valves (inValveClose & outValveClose) and keep them closed as long as the pause is requested (expiratoryPauseButton is true). Once the request is withdrawn, the valves can open again. The property is formulated to only apply while in PCV or PSV modes, focusing on when the user initiates an expiratory pause through the GUI, and requires both valves to remain closed until the pause is stopped by the GUI. The boolean expressions use only the given atomic propositions, notably: 'PCVMode' and 'PSVMode' for modes, 'expiratoryPauseButton' for the GUI request, 'expiratoryPhaseEnd' for expiration ending, and the valve statuses.

decision3_substring: if the Expiratory Pause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will close until the expiratory pause is stopped by the GUI.
